agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is being scoped for customer-facing delivery visibility. Two internal stakeholder teams have issued directly conflicting, non-negotiable requirements regarding whether a courier's real-time location should be exposed to customers.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Shipment Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"A driver or delivery person contracted or employed to physically transport a parcel from origin to destination."},{"term":"Real-time location","definition":"GPS-derived latitude/longitude coordinates of a courier, updated at frequent intervals during an active delivery."},{"term":"Delivery","definition":"The fulfillment lifecycle from dispatch to proof-of-delivery at the recipient's address."},{"term":"Active delivery window","definition":"The time period from courier dispatch to delivery completion, during which tracking may be relevant to the customer."},{"term":"Location granularity","definition":"The precision level at which a courier's position is communicated — ranging from exact GPS coordinates to approximate zone or ETA-only."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A customer may only receive courier location information for a delivery that is assigned to them and currently in an active delivery window.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Exposing location data for deliveries not belonging to the requesting customer or outside the active window is a privacy and data-integrity violation.","testable_assertion":"Given a delivery D assigned to customer C, when customer C2 (C2 ≠ C) requests tracking for D, then the system returns 403 Forbidden."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Courier location data must not be persisted or made available after proof-of-delivery is recorded.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Retaining post-delivery location data has no business purpose and creates privacy and regulatory liability.","testable_assertion":"Given delivery D is marked delivered at time T, when any party queries courier location for D at T+1, then no location data is returned."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"The level of location granularity shown to the customer is a policy decision that must be explicitly configured — exact GPS, approximate zone, or ETA-only.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is the core policy at the center of the stakeholder conflict. A VP or cross-functional decision can change the granularity level.","testable_assertion":"Given the system granularity setting is set to 'zone', when a customer views tracking, then the UI displays an approximate area (e.g., 500m radius) rather than exact coordinates."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Couriers must be informed of what location data is shared with customers and must have acknowledged the data-sharing policy.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Labor law, privacy regulations, and ethical workforce practices require informed consent for location surveillance. Without acknowledgment the system is in a legally corrupt state.","testable_assertion":"Given a courier has not acknowledged the location-sharing policy, when they are dispatched for a delivery, then no location data is shared with the customer for that delivery."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A delivery is in an active delivery window and assigned to the requesting customer","when":"The customer opens the tracking view","then":"The system displays courier progress information at whatever granularity the resolved policy dictates (exact GPS, approximate zone, or ETA-only)"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A delivery has been completed (proof-of-delivery recorded)","when":"The customer attempts to view courier location","then":"No location data is displayed; the UI shows delivery-complete status only"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"The location-sharing granularity is configured as 'approximate zone'","when":"The customer views the tracking map","then":"The courier's position is shown as a fuzzy region (e.g., 500m radius circle), not a precise pin"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A courier has not acknowledged the current location-sharing policy","when":"A customer tracks that courier's delivery","then":"The system falls back to ETA-only mode and does not share any spatial location data"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"The system is in 'ETA-only' granularity mode","when":"A customer views the tracking page","then":"Only the estimated time of arrival is shown with no map or spatial indicator of the courier's position"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier GPS signal is lost mid-delivery","expected_behavior":"System shows last known approximate zone or falls back to ETA-only; never displays stale exact coordinates as if they are current."},{"scenario":"Customer refreshes tracking page at extremely high frequency","expected_behavior":"Rate limiting applies; location updates are throttled to a fixed interval (e.g., every 30 seconds) regardless of client polling."},{"scenario":"Courier is on a multi-stop route serving multiple customers","expected_behavior":"Each customer sees only progress relevant to their own delivery; the system must not reveal other stop locations or sequencing that could identify other customers' addresses."},{"scenario":"Delivery is reassigned to a different courier mid-transit","expected_behavior":"Tracking seamlessly switches to the new courier's data; previous courier's location is immediately purged from the customer's view."},{"scenario":"Courier opts out of location sharing in a jurisdiction where consent is required","expected_behavior":"System defaults to ETA-only for all of that courier's deliveries; no spatial data is shared."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Getting this wrong in either direction is costly. Over-sharing location data drives courier attrition (recruitment and training costs per driver are significant). Under-sharing may reduce customer satisfaction and competitive differentiation. The resolution must balance both revenue retention and workforce cost.","operational":"Courier retention directly impacts delivery capacity. A 10% increase in driver turnover from surveillance concerns could create service-level failures in peak periods. Conversely, zero visibility increases customer support call volume ('where is my package?').","user":"Customers expect some delivery visibility — it is now table-stakes in the industry. However, exact GPS coordinates are not necessarily the expectation; approximate progress or ETA transparency can satisfy the core need without full surveillance.","risk":"High privacy and labor-law risk. GDPR, local labor surveillance laws, and workforce consent requirements make unrestricted GPS sharing legally hazardous. Simultaneously, promising transparency and under-delivering risks brand damage.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Desire delivery visibility; exact coordinates vs. approximate zone vs. ETA-only affects perceived service quality."},{"group":"Couriers","impact":"Directly affected by surveillance level; exact GPS sharing creates a retention and morale problem confirmed by Operations data."},{"group":"Customer Success team","impact":"Owns customer satisfaction KPIs; needs a transparency story to sell, but 'exact GPS' may not be the only viable story."},{"group":"Operations team","impact":"Owns courier retention and fleet capacity; has concrete evidence of driver attrition linked to location sharing."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Not yet consulted but critically affected — location-sharing policy must comply with privacy and labor regulations."},{"group":"Product leadership","impact":"Must arbitrate the policy decision on granularity level; this is not a technical decision."}]},"confidence":{"level":35,"reasoning":"Two non-negotiable stakeholder requirements directly contradict each other. Until product leadership arbitrates the granularity policy and Legal/Compliance weighs in on regulatory constraints, no implementation can be considered correct. The spec as given is internally inconsistent.","high_confidence_areas":["The domain model for delivery tracking is well-understood","Privacy constraints around post-delivery data purging are clear","Courier consent is a hard invariant regardless of resolution","Customer scoping (only see your own delivery) is unambiguous"],"low_confidence_areas":["The correct granularity level cannot be determined without executive arbitration","Regulatory requirements for courier location sharing in relevant jurisdictions are unstated","Whether couriers are employees or contractors affects applicable labor surveillance law"],"assumptions":["Industry norms suggest approximate-zone or ETA-based tracking can satisfy customer transparency needs without exact GPS","Operations team's claim about driver attrition is backed by data, not anecdotal","No regulatory or legal review has been completed for this feature"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires exact GPS coordinates visible to customers at all times during delivery. Operations requires courier location to never be shown to customers. Both positions are stated as non-negotiable. They are mutually exclusive as stated.","affected_stakeholders":["Customer Success","Operations","Couriers","Customers","Product Leadership"],"options":["A: Show exact GPS (satisfies Customer Success, violates Operations requirement, risks courier attrition)","B: Show no location data (satisfies Operations, violates Customer Success requirement, removes competitive differentiator)","C: Compromise — show approximate zone or ETA-only (partially satisfies both, requires both teams to soften their non-negotiable stance)","D: Make granularity configurable per-market or per-courier-consent-level (most flexible, highest implementation cost)"],"recommendation":"Option C or D. Industry standard (UberEats, DoorDash, Amazon) is approximate location, not exact GPS. This satisfies customer transparency needs while materially reducing courier surveillance concerns. Recommend Product Leadership convene both teams, present industry benchmarks, and mandate a granularity policy. Legal/Compliance MUST review before any location sharing is implemented."},{"type":"contradiction","description":"Legal and Compliance have not been consulted on courier location-sharing policy despite this being a workforce surveillance and data privacy decision. Their input is required to resolve the contradiction within regulatory bounds.","affected_stakeholders":["Legal/Compliance","Couriers","Product Leadership"],"options":["Proceed with approximate-zone as a privacy-safer default pending legal review","Block all implementation until legal review is complete"],"recommendation":"Block customer-facing location features until Legal/Compliance provides a written opinion on permissible granularity levels in all operating jurisdictions. Implement the data pipeline and consent infrastructure now, as those are required regardless of resolution."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Cannot implement exact GPS sharing as specified by Customer Success","business_rule_broken":"Operations team's non-negotiable requirement prohibits any courier location display; courier attrition data supports this as a real business risk."},{"violation":"Cannot implement zero location visibility as specified by Operations","business_rule_broken":"Customer Success's non-negotiable requirement demands full GPS transparency as a competitive differentiator."},{"violation":"No implementation can proceed without resolving the stakeholder contradiction","business_rule_broken":"Contradictory non-negotiable requirements from peer teams make any single implementation incorrect by definition. Executive arbitration is required."}]}

content.thinking:
Clear contradiction between two stakeholder teams. Let me analyze and produce the JSON output.

Escalation type: contradiction (two explicit requirements directly conflict)
Confidence: <= 50
